reviews.title: RUN AWAY from this hotel and NEVER LOOK BACK
reviews.text:
RUN AWAY from this accommodation.
room is slightly musky and there are mosquito in the room. there's no way for them to leave with dark nooks and a little window towards the inner walkway. 
the first night was hard.
shower area flooded and no ventilation for the toilet.
the smell filled the bed room.
4 person in a 4 person room.
there wasn't much space to run away after each person used the toilet.
The main room door is not able to lock from the inside. WORST FEAR!
we check out the next morning, booked for 3 nights. it was a nightmare.
hotel didn't agree to refund the remaining 2 nights.
Agoda tried to assist, but hotel holding up their policy to not refund the remaining night
Glad we left and book another place with river mouth view for the same price.
NEVER TO COME INTO THIS HOTEL AGAIN.
Bad basic facilities.
this is my first time to leave a hotel after 1 night in my 20 years of travelling.

reviews.title: Excellent
reviews.negatives: A/C was a bit noisy and blew too strong

reviews.title: Acceptable
reviews.text: Overall not good

reviews.title: Regret and more regrets
reviews.text: It was a horrific experience staying with CY. The room we were given was flooding after a shower. The toilet light was not working well and the aircon was so noisy that I could not sleep well at night. I told the staff those problems but only the toilet flooding was solved. The bed was also so uncomfortable. The hotel is now old and tired and needs serious attention. I could help but feel sorry for myself and regrets booking my stay with CY.

reviews.title: Worn out room
reviews.text: The room definitely need refurbishment. A little dirty.

reviews.title: Old and badly maintained
reviews.text: Hotel was good when  new but is not old and badly maintained. Molly… dusty… bad man odour at lobby and on beds.   Bathroom moldy and things falling apart.  Staff friendly.  Parking need to stop by lobby where the bellboy would ride with you to locked area 180m away to park.  Good thing is that the area is locked and fenced.  No fear of your car getting stolen.

reviews.title: Staff were friendly
reviews.text: Place is full of dust, facilities not maintained, toilet flush no working, power points loose on wall

reviews.title: Nothing Special
reviews.text: Read hotel reviews prior to taking the risk to stay here.  Mentally prepared the worst.  Booked 2 nights at Courtyard The Heeren.  If you like those old Peranakan concept, yes no harm trying to stay here.  But except poor furnitures & fixtures.  Reception consider friendly.  Need to use key to go in & out of the hotel.  Location is fine near jonker market.  Cleanliness need to improve.  I came back with my door ajar coz the room attendant after touching up my room did not close properly.  Reported to front desk.  Those pictures taken shown obvious cracks here & there.  Toilet stains and shower fixtures wobbly, shower door close with a loud cracking sound.  Hope they don’t experience earthquake coz building shown obvious cracked.  Bed rather low and I always kicked at the bed corner. Bed linen looks cleaned but felt dusty.. that’s my personal feeling.  Floor tiles shown obvious pop up coz while walking on tiles you feel tiles popping up.. suggest you bringing slippers along for comfort walking in room.  Although the provide “paper” type of slipper.. no advisable to use it.  Take note.  No carpark available.  You need to park at you own risk around the vicinity.   I have set my lowest standard for picking this hotel coz I like Peranakan concept.  No wow factors and I am out most of the time.  Btw.. room furnitures and fixtures rather dusty.  If you are allergies to dust.. this is not a place for you.

reviews.title: Acceptable
reviews.text: The only good feedback is the location of the hotel. Very accessible to Jonker Street.

reviews.title: Antique but dusty
reviews.text: the room covered with smell and dust.  However, the room was tastefully decorated by the local culture furniture.

reviews.title: Other than location...
reviews.text: ...and friendly staff feel place rather run down with worn bedsheets with holes, broken floor tiles, dust and spider webs along wall, poor ventilation in room(aircon at 16degC feels like fan mode) and bathroom glass door stiff/crank to open/close.

reviews.title: In need of TLC
reviews.text: I stayed in this hotel previously and it was good but this time round, it was disappointing. There were molds on toilet walls. The shower head was falling off. The pillows were terrible. Bed was very uncomfortable. Everything is rundown. Desperately in need of some TLC. Breakfast was horrible. The only plus point was its location.

reviews.title: Reminder to myself: ban
reviews.text: Wall of few room are moldy, guess didn’t do water proof outside wall. Bed not comfy, 2 tv channel only

reviews.title: Not Fantastic
reviews.text: Read many negative reviews from Tripadvisor.  Bringing down standard to book 2 nights stay here. One staff at reception to do checkin.  Room assigned on level 2 and need to walk up stairs.  I like the Peranakan decor.  Booked a deluxe room.  Looks good but realised furniture especially fixtures need to be fixed.  Bed linen felt dusty and surrounding also filled with dust.  Room amenities not fantastic? The wall shown obvious water marks and cracks.  Flooring dirty and pop up tiles.  Advice to bring your own slippers.  When return the next morning room door was ajar.. reported to front desk.  The room door cannot opened nor close properly.  This hotel will not be good for people with OCD.  Room rate reasonable. Low bed frame, hit on the bed corner several times.  You need keys to go in & out of hotel.  No car park facilities,  you will be advise to park outside those empty space.$

reviews.title: Lousy hotel
reviews.text: The hotel had problems with the toilet, faulty hair dryer, wallpaper is peeling and the room is not conducive  for a party of 6. But the staff’s service is very good so we were happy with the attention from them

reviews.title: mixed reviews
reviews.text: The facility was poorly maintained with torn sheets and leaky pipes. But the staff were helpful and supportive, so our experience was quite mixed.

reviews.title: Acceptable
reviews.text:
Location is perfect , walking distance to Jonker street.
Parking is located behind the hotel which is about 5min walk. 
Cleanliness in the room can be improved.

reviews.title: Acceptable
reviews.text: Floor were not that fantasically clean you can't walk around barefooted. Mini fridge has got mould. Some parts of the room has got dust. Pillows are due to be changed too. Car park location is 2 streets away from the hotel at a open car park and it got me worried leaving my car there. However, the only bonus part of it the access to Jonker is a1 street away.

reviews.title: Above average
reviews.text:
my room was covered in dust, the ceiling was covered with cobwebs, the desk and consoles wasn't wiped.

the location is the only good thing, walkable to everything

reviews.title: Won't recommend
reviews.text: Was quite surprised by the maintenance of the room.  It's was old, socket plug casing came off, spider webs hanging on the wall, patches of dust spotted sticking to the wall, case-bearing clothes moth larva spotted (at leave 8 of them) sticking on the wall.  Feedback to the staff and they allowed us to change to deluxe room. Bigger room, socket case is ok. Moth larva still spotted, spider webs still visible. Alot of maintenence need to be done to the rooms I supposed. The only good thing I feel is location. Very near to jonker.  Sorry, but definitely not coming back anymore.

reviews.title: Dirty Little Not so Secret
reviews.text: Atmosphere of common spaces is nice. Staff are friendly.  However, the cleanliness and maintenance of the room leaves a lot to be desired.  Dust in so many nooks and crannies, mold on the wall, large flakes of paint peeling, aircon literally held together with masking tape. They didn’t even properly make-up the room. Not value for money.

reviews.title: The location is great, but hygiene needs improvement.
reviews.text: There’s a strong smell when you first enter the room. It’s more suitable for guests who don’t mind limited toilet privacy — the bed has a clear view of the toilet, and the shower area feels somewhat open-air.

reviews.title: Above average
reviews.text:
The room is dirty and old. Can you imagine few strand of hair on plug and table. And hair dryer with scotch tape. 
Place is quiet and eerie.

reviews.title: Poor condition of room
reviews.text: Quaint heritage hotel with nice interior decor on the ground floor. However, the condition of the family suite on the second floor left us with a bad impression. Several of the lights in the room and bathroom were not in working condition; the high staircase to the beds was steep and dangerous; the inside of the fridge and cups were dirty. Not recommended.

reviews.title: Cleanliness needs to be improved
reviews.text: My family and I stayed in the family room for two nights. Although this property is in a prime location just adjacent to Jonker’s, however, the upkeep, cleanliness and maintenance is very poor in our room. Dusty with a damp odor. Hopefully can improve as the location is very good.

reviews.title: Good but aging
reviews.text: This boutique hotel has seen better days with facilities or items not working in the room. The owner should look at some minor renovations to improve the room quality. It is a no frills hotel just for short stay.

reviews.title: Above average
reviews.text: Less clean, a bit old, but staff is helpful

reviews.title: Above average
reviews.text: Clean hotel with helpful staffs. Near to Jonker Street. Parking provided but its like 10 mins walk from hotel.

reviews.title: rundown and dated
reviews.text:
I stayed in this property years ago and it was unique and looked really cool.
But over the years, the property has not been maintained well, now it looks so dated and old. fixtures and room structure is breaking apart. at least the location is quite good.
staff were also nice and friendly.

reviews.title: Above average
reviews.text: Location was perfect. Breakfast was very delicious. Staff were amazing. Room/toilet needs overhaul and pretty dusty. Also, no fridge in room.

reviews.title: Above average
reviews.text: Ground floor room ceiling water licking

reviews.title: Review
reviews.text:
1. location at a busy street, once miss the location, need to turn a big round, distance is short but traffic is superb heavy
2. hotel carpark was a short distance but unguard, not sure the security is tight
3. room layout was not ideal, toilet and the room were not separated, so what ever happen inside the toilet, can be heard and smell.

reviews.title: Good location
reviews.text: Near to jonker street

reviews.title: Not as impressive
reviews.text: The hotel door is locked all the time n we had to circled around so as not to hold up traffic behind while figuring out where to park n do check in. Location is good though, just one min walk to jonker. Room nothing fancy, no fridge.  Personally, I will not come back here again as there are plenty of boutique hotels within jonker.

reviews.title: Need Maintenance
reviews.text: The location is good and the property is full of interesting antique furniture. However, the room desperately needs maintenance, (1) the water dripping sound at the toilet was so loud that woke me up a few times.  The shower head was broken that water was coming out from around the shower head instead of sprays.  Toilet seat was also broken and loose.

reviews.title: dusty bedroom , shelves, window blind n stairs . Broken window blinds n shower heads
reviews.text: Require thorough cleaning in this room m rectify those malfunctioning items.  Repaint the bedroom wall

reviews.title: Above average
reviews.text: i felt that better maintenance of the place is needed.  Heater was not really working as the water is not hot, toothbrush holder was dirty. bathroom glass had water stains from previous stay? towels looks old. There were mosquitoes in the room and i was bitten quite badly on my 2nd night. breakfast food was quite good but the area was full of mosquitoes as well.  The dustbin along the corrider were not emptied after 2 days. The only good thing was the convenient location near jonker.

reviews.title: Good
reviews.text: Nice location

reviews.title: Near Jonker only
reviews.text: Best is location only but others just ok. Boutique hotel is very old and many need improvements.

reviews.title: malacca stay
reviews.text: pleasant stay but insufficient and inappropriate powerpoint is an issue.

reviews.title: Room
reviews.text: The bed frame has a funny  size, I was hit by it a few times when I get down from bed. Not much tv programe choices to choose. All the switch are old & side desk is lower than the bed.

reviews.title: Only if staying near Jonker Street is what you want
reviews.text:
Staff were definitely very friendly and helpful and that's the one big plus point besides being very near Jonker Street -- just one street away.  They accommodated my request to have early check-in and late check-out, helped me to get a grab, arranged a room such that I didn't have any mozzie issues. 

The downside is, there was practically no facilities to talk about, not even hot water or an oven to heat up food. 

The hotel seriously needs some long overdue maintenance although my room was acceptably clean.  The place does look aged.

reviews.title: Not so pleasant
reviews.text:
Booked triple room.

Inner quilt , high time to sun bath it - damp feeling.

Quilt cover has many holes. Bedsheet looks clean but not after lying on it.

The bench in washroom in shaking condition.

Mini fridge within part of washroom - not right place. 

Bath room floor and corner of wall  mould forming up or already formed.

The faceplate  of electrical
Socket loose or just not flush to wall(village standard)  dare not use .

reviews.title: Good stay
reviews.text: We had a good stay in malacca. The location is just nice, and they have a car park if you drive a car to malacca

reviews.title: Good
reviews.text:
Sink stuck and first time shower no water.
not feel good with mirror at the headboard.

reviews.title: Great location and friendly staff but think twice if you are staying with children
reviews.text: Great location with direct accessibility to Jonker walk. Parking is approximately half a kilometre walk away but you can drop your luggages on the front door before parking your car at the designated car park. Think twice if you are traveling with children who will be moving around. Family rooms are located on the first floor with very thin flooring. Children moving about in the room will create noise to the tenant downstairs. There shouldn't be any problem if you are traveling without children and require just sleeping at night without additional facilities.

reviews.title: Did not meet expectations
reviews.text:
My husband and I booked this hotel based on such positive reviews.  Unfortunately, we were disappointed.

Pros: Staff was very kind, fantastic location, nicely decorated. 

Cons: The room layout. There is not a full wall separating the sleeping area from the bathroom. Because of the lack of wall, outside light shines in at all times through the bathroom window (which is the only window by the way).  We also have never stayed at a hotel with worse soundproofing. We were woken up by a family yelling in the middle of the night.  It sounded like they were in the room with us.

reviews.title: Good
reviews.text: The location is good becuse its near Jonker Street. The Family Suite is quite rundown and there is only toilet on the ground level which is inconvenient for a group.

reviews.title: Good
reviews.text: Location is convenient, but not so clean, and got some wet smell

reviews.title: great location
reviews.text:
great location, short walking distance to jonker street.
the only issue for me is the room is so clean

reviews.title: It's not worth living with this kind of equipment at this price.
reviews.text: I live in the room upstairs and there is almost no water in the shower at night have to wait for a while for the water to gradually come in & the washbasin incoming water is big and small, the water sprays everywhere. It's not worth living with this kind of equipment at this price.

reviews.title: Room needs refurbishment moldy smell and noisy
reviews.text: Poor sound proofing met with inconsiderate guests in the morning. Could not sleep in

reviews.title: Good
reviews.text: The staff that helped us checked in did not informed us the hotel parking is available. And we have to spent extra money for overnight parking at the public parking area.

reviews.title: Good
reviews.text: Parking is a big problems.....too expensive for the small room and no facilities at all...

reviews.title: Needs maintenance
reviews.text: Location was good as it was a short distance to Jonker St which is the main area to look around & it is close to other places of interest. The hotel was quite lovely and updated with a quaint peranakan feel about it. H'ever, being an old shophouse, it looks like they have problems with rising damp and the paint on the walls were all peeling and the walls themselves were cracking. General maintenance seem to be lacking and things were looking worse for wear.

reviews.title: Good location
reviews.text: Location and room is good. The only setback is the car-park which is quite far from the hotel. Another issue if u get dowstriat room..u can hear upstriat guest walk sound bit annoying ...The breakfast is good and staffs is friendly.

reviews.title: First time stay
reviews.text: The bed is not stable. The floor seems unstable and not even also. The toilet is big though and strangely there is a gap at the side which allows people to see. So not convenient if staying w friends. There are some ants around so have to be careful with your food. Don't place near your bed. Understand is old house with peranakan style so not expecting high standard but not worth the money overall.

reviews.title: No free parking, worn towels
reviews.text: As we drove to Melaka, our key criteria is free parking. Listing state that free parking for guest, but was told by the staff rudely that they already updated other platform. But not on Agoda. Hinting it’s as though it’s our fault that we book via Agoda. Then the towels are worn out with holes.

reviews.title: Value for money
reviews.text: Location is amazing. Walking distance to many tourist attractions. However no parking available if you’re driving.

reviews.title: Great stay!
reviews.text:
Great central location, pretty Nyonya style lobby. 

Room was a little dusty and could be maintained better. But overall would stay again!

reviews.title: Good
reviews.text: Good location but no guest parking available onsite.

reviews.title: booked the family room
reviews.text:
Superb location. 
can accommodate big families of up to at least 8 pax. 
Cleanliness can be improved. quite dusty on the the curtains/windows and furnitures.  Bedsheets are OK, but theres some stains.

reviews.title: Strategic location
reviews.text: Strategic location at Jonker street, easy access to all shops n restaurants, rooms needed to touch up and refurbish a little as I can see clearly the water leaking stains

reviews.title: Great Location
reviews.text: Wished parking was nearer to hotel. Great location. Room is huge, though a bit dated.

reviews.title: located near Jonker street
reviews.text: interesting place to stay if you are a cultural fanatic. the hotel is built in the old building with Peranakan concept. beware of mosquito, toilet status can be improved as it seemed quite open.

reviews.title: Normal Lodge
reviews.text: Nothing special

reviews.title: Cleanliness need improvement or else location is really good
reviews.text: The location of this hotel is ideal for free and easy traveller. It is strategically located near Jonker Street, just a stone throw away from the night market. Room is quiet but the bedsheet and pillow was dirty with stain on it.

reviews.title: Good location and easy to access
reviews.text: The place is central and nearby there are a lot of stalls selling food. Either for breakfast or dinner.

reviews.title: Let down by NO Car Park
reviews.text:
Although there is a car park, it is a good walk away. 
When we were checking in, I parked my at a legal spot and went in to check in.  In that short space of time, I got a Parking Ticket !  This spoilt the mood immediately, hence I do not recommend this hotel for those who are driving.

reviews.title: Good location.
reviews.text:
In the old town near the night market.
Great choice of restaurants &cafes in walking distance.
Room could do with some maintenance but all the basic needs are covered.

reviews.title: Good
reviews.text: It was a day trip with our elderly mother.  Location is fantastic! Car park is a bit inconvenient, especially when it rains.  And water was dripping everywhere when it rained, you had to watch out on the slippery floor.  The common bathroom on the ground floor could be better maintained/cleaned.

reviews.title: Nice boutique hotel in the center of Malacca.
reviews.text: Breakfast was excellent, the room nice and in the tourist area. The only problem is if it rains at night. It was too noisy.

reviews.title: beautiful hotel
reviews.text:
great location, beautiful and unique interior, nice breakfast with nice chef 

poor WiFi and phone reception 
very basic room, don't even have refrigerator in room with this rate.! 
and you won't believe it, the TV have only 2 channels !!!

reviews.title: Great location, walking distance to Jonker Street
reviews.text: This hotel is located one street right behind Jonker Street. Car park is available at behind of the hotel. Staff is helpful and responsive. We enjoyed our stay in this hotel, but can be improved if the room is more sound-proof. It could be a disturbance for light sleepers as we could easily hear the footstep sound and talking voice in our room.

reviews.title: The atmosphere and the richness are very good!
reviews.text: The atmosphere of the shared areas and the room's interior were really lovely, so I felt it was a bit of a waste that the room cleaning (especially the water areas) could have been more thorough. The shower drain wasn't very good, and the water tended to pool. (It flows eventually after a while.) Perhaps because it was on the first floor, there were small ants wandering around, even in the pouch I had placed on the desk. However, I was able to stay at a good price, and the atmosphere was nice, so I was happy overall.

reviews.title: Very good
reviews.text: Gentle recommend to change new bed linen and pillow.

reviews.title: Heeren Boutique Hotel
reviews.text:
The location is very strategic with walking distance many of the main attractions.
It does not offer any facilities such as restaurant or parking, but the rate reflects it. The upside is the hotel is very quite, and homely.

reviews.title: Friendly reception, good service
reviews.text:
Nice room at a convenient location but hotel needs to be refurbished because there were cracks and the floor seems dirty.
Friendly, knowledgeable and helpful staff. Overall, a good experience.

reviews.title: Great location
reviews.text: Great location within short walking distance to Jonker street. Other than please consider the other aspect. The hotel needs an update. The floors and walls are really "thin" any movement can be heard.  There is also no free parking at the hotel thru there's a free parking within 8mins walk via the back door.  With the price we paid, mostly is due to the location.

reviews.title: Very heritage hotel
reviews.text: Reception display lots of antique items and bedroom also design the old fashion display but no hanger to hang your cloths or closet. Room not properly clean especially the corners of the beds. Hotel within walkable around Jonker street. Only Friday, Sat & Sun street filled with many food vendors.

reviews.title: Decent hotel
reviews.text: Excellent & convenient location, facilities average, old hotel, faulty items, friendly & helpful staff, excellent service

reviews.title: Pleasant Stay, Though Facilities Need Updating
reviews.text: Overall, it was a nice place to stay, but there are a few areas that could be improved. The room door still uses a traditional key lock, which doesn’t feel very secure. It was also quite noisy along the corridor whenever people walked by. Many of the facilities are quite old and would benefit from upgrades. Despite these issues, the stay was generally pleasant.

reviews.title: Very good
reviews.text:
Location is very convenient, less than 3 mins walk to Jonker Street

Price -  slightly high

Hotel - abit rundown, will be good if they do a liitle bit of renovation

Bed - is comfortable and decent 

Security - You need to have an access card into the hotel lobby.  However the door lock to the room seems to break anytime .

Toilet - reasonably clean, hot water ok, suggest to bring your own towels 

Overall - 7.5/10

reviews.title: Pleasant enough stay
reviews.text: Conveniently located in the heart of Jonker Walk. Rooms were dusty, towels and bedsheets were old, stained and slightly smelly. Basic toiletreies - dont expect too much. T It is like paying for the convenience but expect hostel level amenities. Staff were lovely though.

reviews.title: Great Location
reviews.text: Great location, with parking provided (but not within hotel vicinity). Used to come around 10 years ago and it is definitely showing the age a little..

reviews.title: Great place to stay with value
reviews.text: The  room is fills with nice scent.

reviews.title: Room
reviews.text: Rm 5 Door not secure, toilet should have more privacy. The shower does not have a stand to put our toiletries stuff. Aircon was not cold. Although whole rm  & pillow is clean but got some kind of smell . The staff are friendly & late check up which we didn’t expect that. Suggest that they need a reno for all the room. TV remote is not working well.

reviews.title: Historical Tour
reviews.text: Staying in a heritage building. Very memorable stay. Cleanliness can be improved

reviews.title: Not worth the price
reviews.text: Great location, lovely staff, but wouldn't recommend due to lack of maintenance, mouldy room and uncomfortable bed.

reviews.title: Yes for convenience
reviews.text: Travellers have to moderate expectations when staying here. It's super convenient when bulk of activities is around red square Jonker street, all within 10 mins walk. Save travel time, taxi fare. But families with strollers or old folks beware, many steps and some rooms are on second floor. We took the family suite which was 6 beds on #2 and 2 beds on #1, the spiral staircase super narrow and only one bath on #1 which was very dangerous for young kids and old folks. I had to pay extra to set up two beds in living room for the oldest and youngest just to feel at peace ..

reviews.title: Great location
reviews.text: Superb location but… cockroaches! This place does not have a boutique status.  Female staff exceptional, the rest are probably sleeping behind the counter.

reviews.title: Good but not value to money
reviews.text: There are other better alternatives

reviews.title: Good location, nice place
reviews.text: Everything was good. Parking is 2 mins walk away from the premise. Everything in the room worked fine. Staff were helpful too. However , breakfast was a bit of downer, just don’t expect too much.

reviews.title: Excellent Location
reviews.text: It's nice to stay in this boutique hotel..However, due to wear and tear, the toilet room can not be shut tight. The staff taught us the walkaround. My dad expect to have more TV channels on air..He has been disappointed as only 2 channels available.  Luckily, it's bearable just for one night.

reviews.title: Very good
reviews.text: Location was excellent, and staff were very helpful and accommodating. However,  aspects such as mosquitoes in the rooms and courtyard, as well as hot water running out in the middle of the shower marred what would otherwise be an excellent experience.

reviews.title: Excellent location offer great convenience for exploring the area on foot but mosquitoes & awkward bathroom design were major annoyance...
reviews.text:
Near Jonker Walk & Dutch Square, easy to walk to most places of attraction.  

Lots of mosquitoes in room which was annoying...  Bathroom design should be improved for greater privacy.

reviews.title: Goods
reviews.text: Very nice

reviews.title: Excellent location, basic amenities melded with authentic heritage
reviews.text: Excellent location along Heeren Street. Rooms and bathrooms could do with a sprucing up. Good value. Serviceable for a couple nights' stay.

reviews.title: In the midst of history, culture and food
reviews.text: Revisited after many years, including a pandemic. The place has maintained its charm, albeit in need of some maintenance and facelift. Best part about the stay is the kind front desk, an older gentleman, and the location!

reviews.title: Beautiful accommodation, cleanliness is so so.
reviews.text: The accommodation was pretty and in a good location. The counter and courtyard space were decorated in a very antique style, which I liked, but there was a lot of dust in the room, and it had a bit of a moldy smell. There were windows, but they could hardly be opened... Maybe it was because it was on the first floor. As someone who values cleanliness and ventilation, I found it a bit uncomfortable, but I think it would be a nice place for those who prioritize atmosphere and aesthetics. The night market and other spots were also nice and convenient to walk to!

reviews.title: Good location only
reviews.text: The room quite run down & musky smell.

reviews.title: Courtyard Hereen Melaka
reviews.text:
Drop off point too near the main road. Traffic congestion , unsafe for children. 

Towels and bed sheets are old with torn holes and ware. 

Friendly staffs and cleanliness is excellent.

reviews.title: Nice place
reviews.text:
Close to Jonker. Room is big, bed is comfy, sheets are spotless. Space is tastefully furnished and renovated. Kept the heritage vibe without it feeling too dated. 
No parking on site, but guest parking isn't too far away. 
Hot water took a while to heat up and some sockets weren’t working properly. 
Would return.

reviews.title: Great location
reviews.text: Location of hotel is great, few mintues walk to Jonker street, room aircon is cold

reviews.title: LOCATION IS GOOD
reviews.text: The hotel should plan for refurbished. Look old on furniture

reviews.title: LOCATION IS GOOD
reviews.text: The boutique hotel looks old inside.

reviews.title: Strategic Location
reviews.text: Location is strategic, can walk to Jonker Street easily. The only setback is no parking provided. You need to park far & pay the the meter parking.

reviews.title: Good location
reviews.text: Good location. parking far, especially if girls walk at night, quite dangerous. Old style hotel. Nice staff. Clean.

reviews.title: Family suite
reviews.text: I stayed in the family suite and loved the space as well as the location. The staff was friendly and helpful. The parking was located behind the premise, a short walk away, not within the building itself.

reviews.title: Nice heritage room but needs maintenance
reviews.text: Location is superb - right beside Jonker street.  Family suite is spacious and provides a unique heritage vibe. However the property definitely needs regular maintenance. I’m not a hygiene freak but spider cobs at corners and dirty mini fridge spells a need for better maintenance.

reviews.title: Air condition is noisy
reviews.text: Air condition noisy

reviews.title: Very good
reviews.text: Location of this hotel is superb if you are planning to be in Jonker street.

reviews.title: Very good
reviews.text: Will need to bring your own extension cord if you have various devices as hotel don't provide and rooms do not have enough socket.  Do note, room door are locked by keys, no key card.  No lift as it's a 2 storey boutique hotel.  Staff were helpful and friendly.  I like it when towels were changed daily, shower gel and shampoo replenished provided and replenished daily .   Location fantastic, as it is just less than 5 mins walk to Jonker Street.

reviews.title: Just being different from other hotels
reviews.text: Nice local antique decorated hotel with modern facilities located within short walking diistance to most tourist attractions in UNESCO Malacca. Lots of cafes, restaurants and food street- Jonker Walk. Fabulous experience to be so near to where the fun is.

reviews.title: Very special staycation
reviews.text: The room design is full of the baba nyonya style and the receptionist is very helpful and friendly.

reviews.title: Nice Old School Charm
reviews.text: Nice location and facilities for basic needs. My only complain would be there are no cable channels for the little ones in tow. Overall, good and charming basic hotel 👍🏻

reviews.title: Very peranakan feel
reviews.text: Environment of the whole hotel is very vintage. Exactly same as picture. 2mins walk to jonker street. However sound proofing may need to be improve. Other than that all is good!

reviews.title: Heritage Feel
reviews.text:
Baba Nyonya hotel.
Felt the room may need a bit of touch up
Room is spacious and hotel provides parking
Is located at Jonker, walking distance to night market.
Staff is friendly
Has no lift. Need to use stairs, but as I am on backpack only, is fine

reviews.title: Overall A Pleasant Stay
reviews.text: I was expecting a little more from this place given the theme and decorations of the hotel. The lobby certainly gave a warm Nyonya style welcome but as we entered our rooms, some of the facilities (i.e. toilet, bed, lighting) were not very well maintained and not well cleaned either. I only hope when the pandemic is fully over, there would be more funds for the hotel to refurbish the items mentioned above.  Overall, the stay was pleasant but could be better ;-)

reviews.title: Good location. Room is just okay.
reviews.text: Location is great because it's only one street behind the night market. Parking lot is located 2 streets behind the hotel. Not too far but quite scary to walk at night. Checking in would be difficult if there's no available street parking. Room was just okay. Staff was very friendly and helpful.

reviews.title: Pros and Cons
reviews.text:
Stayed 1 night during August 2019

Pros
- Good shower facilities with good toiletries provided
- Good location (just behind Jonker Street)
- Small spread but nice breakfast included
- Beautiful lobby

Cons
- Room was changed from Double to Twin sharing without prior notice
- Very thin walls and we can hear outside movements and talking very clearly
- Thin floor which trembles whenever someone walked past outside the room
- No lock function for bathroom/toilet. Door was half transparent which another occupant can just see in
- Only one TV channel was working during our stay
- Windows are all nailed and can't be opened (just for aesthetic purpose).

reviews.title: Awesome Boutique hotel in Jonker street
reviews.text: Superb boutique hotel, I wanted to spend some time around Jonker street and found this awesome hotel. It's been refreshed but was based on the original design of this pre world war 2 house. We took a slight bigger room so paid a little bit more but I was happy paying it. Room was comfortable and loved the design. Each room in this hotel has it's own unique design, so any subsequent visit could be in a differently decorated room. Location was superb, just 1 row behind the main Jonker Street.

reviews.title: Great location and atmospheric building
reviews.text:
Best location in quiet street behind Jonker.
Room is a nice size and comfy bed.
Outside room areas are very pretty.
The room was not as clean as it could be, especially the shower.
Breakfast is not great, not much selection.
For the price it is good, could be excellent if they gave the rooms a good clean and put on a better breakfast.

reviews.title: Excellent value for money.
reviews.text: The Peranakan decor adds to the experience unique to City of Malacca.

reviews.title: Great location
reviews.text:
Great location and spacious room. 
Nice decorations
Breakfast is delicious.

reviews.title: Very good
reviews.text: Near to Jonker Walk. Breakfast was ok. Just that there were a few hours where there were no power supply due to work by the authorities.

reviews.title: good location
reviews.text: hotel is unique,but loads of mosquito sleeping with us and the toilet design not very suitable for family,like the breakfast and location

reviews.title: Good but need maintenance
reviews.text: We've stayed in this hotel for many times since it was opened because of the design, breakfast and location (used to be top no.1 boutique hotel in Malacca). However, general maintenance needed. The wall painting is peeling off, the dusty telephone, wrinkly bed linen and pillow cover all need to be taken care of, bathroom door hinge need to be replaced (room F04). Car park is quite far from the hotel but is still acceptable. We hope our next visitation will be a better experience  :)

reviews.title: Great Deco and great place to stay for family
reviews.text: Everything is great except cleanliness. The room is dusty especially the on the bed.

reviews.title: Superior Family Room
reviews.text:
Will definitely book this place again for:
1) Proximity of this hotel to Jonker street and the river (very good walk experience day and night) ->no worries about getting grab as you wouldn't need to.
2) Very nice interior design, very good photo background and props.
3) Strong aircon, always love coming back to the hall of the hotel and hotel room.
4)Friendly, helpful and nice staffs.
5) Can wake up and have a quick walk to the back of hotel to see how the small wet market looks like in the morning, approx. 2 mins walk.

However, hope on my next visit these will be better:
1) Superb interior and environment but the scent smells a little stale like undried clothings
2) Key lock not working well, may have to ask staff to help open.
3) Toilet bowl having blue flushed stains (a bit scary) 4) Overhead shower head is spoilt, water spraying out from above the head and not below, but there is still a hand shower head on a loose handle bar which doesn't allow firm catchment when water is on. 
5) water drainage is bad as water starts flooding upon start of shower.

reviews.title: The atmosphere of the building is nice!
reviews.text: It is located on a fairly quiet street, just one road over from John Carstreet, but it was convenient to go anywhere. The building and interior retained the old atmosphere, which I liked. The canopied bed also got me excited. The staff were friendly and it was comfortable. The water facilities were not inconvenient, and the amenities were well-equipped. However, it was disappointing to see that the shower room's blinds were moldy... I think it would be better to just remove them altogether. This was a place I would like to stay at again.

reviews.title: Classic hotel
reviews.text: Always love staying here as the location is right in the middle of Jonker street without being in the crowd

reviews.title: Great Location
reviews.text:
Great location if you want to stay near Jonker Street.
But room is small.
No wardrobe to hang clothes for a family size room.
No fridge in room.
No chair or seat in room.

reviews.title: Great location & lots of character
reviews.text: Great location in the heritage centre but quiet and not in the busy Jonker street which is only 10 mins max walk. This place has character and history however it’s well maintained and clean.

reviews.title: Cosy Peranakan themed boutique hotel
reviews.text: Overall enjoyed our stay. Room, esp toilet could be cleaner.

reviews.title: Great stay
reviews.text: We stayed at the Family Suite and had a great time. With the very steep stairs, may not suit very young kids and old folks. Very functional and unbeatable location. Staff were helpful.

reviews.title: Simple, comfortable and convenient
reviews.text:
Simple setup in the hotel & room, but having the necessary to make our stay feel safe & comfortable.  

Bed is clean and comfortable (1 queen and 1 super single mattress on platform).  Enough tables/bench to put things.  Got place to hang towels, right outside the shower, thus making it very convenient.  Good lighting (bright and enough light control therefore I can turn on minimal light when sleeping).  What's lacking is 13A (for phone charging).  Only got 1 at bedside and the other one is next to sink (which is a bit dangerous to charge phone/boil water).

Hotel is located away from the busy area at jonker street but not too far.  Easy access to food.  Though hotel dun have parking, there is open space parking behind it.  Just few mins walk if enter hotel thru back door.

All in all, we enjoyed the stay at this hotel and we will consider this hotel again if rate is good (in our next melaka trip).  Thanks to the hotel staff for making it a pleasant stay for us.

reviews.title: Basic needs met with good location
reviews.text: Walking distance to all the popular spots in Jonker. Only 1 issue with my room is that most of the plug points are not functioning.

reviews.title: Parking issue
reviews.text: Possible onside

reviews.title: Great location
reviews.text: note that there is no lift here, there is a backdoor which is convenient if you've parked behind. Nathroom did not have actual door, sliding glass with bamboo decoration. Not ideal if you want privacy. staff were friendly, very near jonker, convenient stay

reviews.title: Location is good and value for money
reviews.text: The location is good, room is unique but a bit small for 4 pax. Have elderly in group so it is not so good without elevator.

reviews.title: Yet again another good stay!
reviews.text: Is a vintage hotel so it's an old housing but with lot of memory. Near to Jonker and surrounding with foods and drinks.

reviews.title: Great Location
reviews.text: Have stayed in the past. A little the worse for wear as the years have gone by but still a quaint little hotel and location is great if you're looking to explore the Jonker and nearby areas. Parking available.

reviews.title: Fantastic Location
reviews.text: Albeit the rooms feeling old with those peeled wallpaper, the location is fantastic. I've chose to stay here simply for the location and gorgeous lobby.  The night market is only 2 minutes walk away and great restaurants all within walking distance. Highly recommend if you want to be right in the centre of the hustle bustle. However, don't expect top notch rooms. They were mediocre and even looks worn out. However, for the location as compared to other 3 stars ones... I have got no complaints.

reviews.title: Good location
reviews.text: The hotel is at a very good location if you would like to explore Jonker historical area. Basic necessities like towel, toiletries are provided. Nice comfortable bed too

reviews.title: Value for money
reviews.text: A quaint heritage hotel.  If u looking for a cosy place with good location, this is a great place to stay. This reminds of my childhood house with a long  courtyard, lush green and natural lightings.

reviews.title: Good hotel
reviews.text: Close to sights and Jonker st.  Staff very helpful and breakfast good

reviews.title: Nice hotel good location
reviews.text:
Pro:
Complimentary carpark is available 5 mins walk away 
Right within the tourist spots 
Nice rooms
Friendly staff 

Cons:
If taking downstairs room, be sure to ask if there’s kids or many people occupying upstairs, if so, you will hear LOUD footsteps as the upstairs occupants walk back and forth. Due to the wooden flooring above. I’m not even a light sleeper.

reviews.title: Nice boutique hotel
reviews.text: Nice hotel to take Nonya photo shoot. Location is not bad.

reviews.title: Best location ever
reviews.text: In the heart of Jonker. We could shop n put our things back in the hotel n out again. Especially in the hot hot or wet wet afternoons.

reviews.title: v nice small boutiqe
reviews.text: no real complaints. room smelt a little damp at first and no F&B of any kind but plenty of coffee shops and eateries nearby

reviews.title: Cosy ....
reviews.text: Like the hotel ... nice, clean and cosy.  Very near to Jonker St.

reviews.title: Good location
reviews.text: The place is very clean and the staff is very helpful. Good location  which very near to Jonker Street. They provide free parking near the hotel.

reviews.title: Very good
reviews.text: No lift.

reviews.title: Very good
reviews.text: The location is very convenient esp for those wanting to explore Malacca on foot. Liked the ambience of the hotel. Rooms are clean & staff were very helpful/friendly.

reviews.title: Very good
reviews.text: No breakfast unfortunately

reviews.title: Location & Great Customer Experience
reviews.text: Some of the facilities are worn down but overall it’s a nice property with good location. Superb customer service.  Price on the up side since it’s a peak period when I check in. Keep up the good work.

reviews.title: Heritage Flavour
reviews.text: Heritage flavour hotel..

reviews.title: Great location
reviews.text: Location are very good.

reviews.title: Very good
reviews.text: Lovely deco and ambience. Rooms a little small and no dressing table and chair but very clean. Excellent location.  Small TV with no interesting channels.

reviews.title: Tasteful design of a boutique hotel
reviews.text: The architecture of the boutique hotel itself is the reason for visit and for staying.  You can experience the peranakan culture and architecture especially at the courtyard of the hotel. The room is clean, however the sheets probably need some thorough cleaning or simply replacing them with new ones. The designs of the rooms are rather "simple/basic" and pale in comparison to the colorful and playful courtyard/lobby. The attached bathroom lacks privacy as you can see through the glass door/window from the bedroom to the toilet/shower area - not quite suitable if you go with friends/family members. Overall, the stay is enjoyable and interesting.

reviews.title: Dated Room
reviews.text: Hotel and Room was pretty dated but comfortable none the less. Aircon took some time to power up but once kicked in it remained nice and cool throughout the night. Mattresses was big, nice and comfy for all 4 of us. Location is great as its just a street away from Jonker Walk with many convenience stores and restaurants near by.

reviews.title: Superb location!
reviews.text: The location is amazing, staff is very friendly & treat us well. Bed is nice & comfy but other aspect of the rooms need improvements.

reviews.title: Nice building. Antique and Vintage Nyonya style.
reviews.text:
Excellent location and nice deco.
Just the room is a bit old and need some repair work.
We took 2 couple rooms and located upstairs, so if seniors adults, do ask for room downstairs.

reviews.title: Perfect location
reviews.text: Location is perfect. Good value to stay. A bit old but still fairly clean to stay. Room is missing a few things such as kettle and tissues. Once called they are bringing the stuff quickly.

reviews.title: Overall we had a pleasant experience and the staff is extremely helpful
reviews.text: The staff help us arranged "grab" travel service from Malacca to KL. The only downside is presence of mosquitoes. But they provide us with insect spray n cream when we notify the staff.

reviews.title: Stylish and Almost Perfect
reviews.text: Nice stylish hotel with a great location.  Pity about (1) Wifi that didn't work with my laptop (and one of their staff's phone; mine worked though), and (2) shower area drain that was very   s-l-o-w.

reviews.title: Very good
reviews.text:
Top location, beautiful ambience. This is probably the best looking interior of Malaccan hotels: pure peranakan housing. Other hotels have similar concepts with bad results. 
As it is an historical building, the hotel doesn't have facilities but the room is spacious and nice. Some people will find weird having no real wall separation between bedroom and toilet. You can hear/see mostly everything. 
TV really only has 2 channels.

Take a room on the upper floor.

reviews.title: Very good
reviews.text: Good location, breakfast.

reviews.title: Comfortable
reviews.text:
My wife and I loved the traditional look of the hotel. The room was cosy and romantic. 
It has good access to Jonker Street and all the attractions nearby.

reviews.title: Very good
reviews.text: Lovely heritage hotel with friendly and helpful staff

reviews.title: Uniquely Malacca Hotel
reviews.text: No regret! Not a typical hotel, but hotel with a charming style of Malacca. Strategically located just beside Jonker Street and walking distance to major shopping centers and places of interests.

reviews.title: Lovely property but disappointing room
reviews.text: Lovely boutique hotel. Thing is though hotel room was not well designed. Platform bed with hot water kettle placed at end, meant crawling on knees to get water, no real table top/desk, safe in toilet., no real space to hang clothes.

reviews.title: Nice Boutique Hotel
reviews.text: Its a nice boutique hotel, through the room is a little small for 3 (its the last available room) everything was nice and clean and it was very dainty reflective of the perankan culture of Melaka. Breakfast was great too. Its near Jonker walk :)

reviews.title: Pleasant stay
reviews.text: I pick this hotel due to the location which means that I do not need to drive out for food as most of the famous food stalls are nearby. I love old heritage buildings and being able to stay in one furnished with many Peranakan household items truly feels like home in the olden days. I would love to come back again.

reviews.title: Excellent
reviews.text: Hospitality is fantastic. The place is well maintained & clean. Would've been perfect if there was an additional hook near the shower.

reviews.title: Excellent
reviews.text:
your inputs:
Great Staff and Clean, Convenient Stay
The staff were exceptionally helpful and friendly, which made the stay enjoyable. The location was convenient and the facilities met expectations, contributing to good overall value. Cleanliness was maintained well, although there is slight room for improvement in some areas. Overall, a solid choice for a comfortable and pleasant visit.

reviews.title: Excellent location
reviews.text: Excellent location 10 mins walk to jonker street

reviews.title: Nyonya style
reviews.text: It is really an aunthetic nyonya style stay (the exterior and interior), cleanliness is not so great for our room as in there's dust at most of the furniture, and the fridge is very dirty (didn't use it anyway). For the price, it is good. For location, it's in Jonketr street so expect traffic jam within, there's no free car park. But you get to take nyonya photo and brag to your friends 😂

reviews.title: Beautiful hotel, friendly staff and very clean
reviews.text: Beautifully decorated rooms around green courtyards in a great location. Staff was friendly and helpful. Room was very clean.

reviews.title: Good
reviews.text: Staff is very nice good location room is comfortable will come back again

reviews.title: Located just behind Jonker Street
reviews.text: Only for sleeping, nothing else. Nice small hotel with old fashioned decor.

reviews.title: A Charming Stay That Feels Like a Step Back in Time
reviews.text:
We booked this place mainly for its antique building, and it definitely delivered on that old-school charm! The whole place gives off strong nostalgic vibes — from the creaky wooden floors to the 2000s TV and rotary phone in the room. Yes, it’s old, but everything still works surprisingly well. Hot water pressure was strong, and nothing felt broken, just aged in a nice way.

The room was a bit dusty, but honestly, nothing unexpected for a heritage-style building. It actually added to the "living museum" feel of the place. The staff were super friendly which waited up for us until almost midnight for check-in and even held our luggage after check-out so we could explore more.

One of the highlights was renting the Nyonya traditional outfit in town and taking photos around the hotel. It’s honestly the perfect backdrop — beautiful, authentic, and best part? No crowds photobombing you like at the Baba museum, and no extra photo fees either!

Location-wise, it couldn’t be better. Right in the heart of Jonker, everything is within walking distance.

Overall, it’s not a luxury hotel, but if you’re looking for a cozy, traditional, and photo-worthy experience, this is a great pick. Would stay again for the vibes alone!

reviews.title: Perfect hotel for 3
reviews.text: My 2 friends and I wanted a 2 day 1 night getaway in Malacca and Heeren Courtyard is the only hotel that offers a reasonable hotel for a family superior room which could accommodate the 3 of us which was perfect and also we wanted a hotel which is very near Jonker Street.

reviews.title: Courtyard Heeren Hotel Melaka
reviews.text: I booked this room for my friend and her son from the US. They complained of mosquitoes and bed bugs. They have a lot of bug bites on their arms and legs. The shower area leaked and water flowed to the toilet area. It was not convenient to use the toilet.

reviews.title: Heritage Charm Hotel
reviews.text:
2nd time stay at this hotel as its located just steps from Jonker Street.  Check-in was smooth and front desk staff is friendly too.  Love the deco at the hotel lobby with rich peranakan displays.  

Beautifully preserved heritage hotel, rooms is spacious, however, climbing up and down the spiral staircase was scary, not recommended for elderly.  There's a room with 1 queen size bed on the first level,  bathroom is also on the first level and on the second level, there's only 3 queen size bed.  Some traffic noise from the road as the room is facing the road.

Overall, a very pleasant stay.

reviews.title: Good location
reviews.text:
We loved  the location. It’s only 2 mins walk from Case Del Rio hotel and in the vicinity of Jonker street. So convenient. There are many massage shops and F&B shops around. Mins walk to the insta cafe-The Daily Fix and the famous Peranakan Mansion.
Only minus is that there is no convenience shop nearby. Have to walk app 8 mins to the nearest one.

reviews.title: Best location
reviews.text: Best location and comfortable place to stay

reviews.title: Great Location from Jonker Walk
reviews.text: Less than one street away from the main Jonker Walk. The staircase to the 2nd flr is very steep though, not suitable for older ppl

reviews.title: Excellent
reviews.text: Nice location

reviews.title: Near Jonker and carpark
reviews.text: The hotel is located nearby to the open carpark which is easily access via back door. Very near to Jonker street and can easily explore around there. Would appreciate if the hotel consider to change all the pillows to improve the image and sleep quality

reviews.title: Good location
reviews.text:
Good location 
Walking distance to Jonker walk.
Nice place, free of parking.
Friendly staff.

reviews.title: Good value for money and great location
reviews.text: Overall very good stay but the facilities are a tadbit old and needs better maintenance

reviews.title: Exceptional location at heritage hotel
reviews.text: Pleasant stay at the boutique hotel. Exceptional location. Nice eating place nearby. The Baboon house is just beside the hotel. Quiet environment. Down side: WiFi wasn't strong, bathroom drain was choked but was cleared by helpful staff.

reviews.title: Excellent
reviews.text: Very close to Jonker Street. A beautiful hotel. Am glad that they also provide free hotel parking for guests who drive.

reviews.title: Our Go-to Hotel for Malacca Jonker Walk
reviews.text: Have always stayed in this hotel during our stay in Malacca. Location was superb, right in the heart of Jonker Walk. Love the vibes of the hotel, very peranakan style, with a different design for each room. Pricing for the rooms are very affordable. Hubby and parents love it a lot.

reviews.title: Excellent
reviews.text: Nice and cosy place located very near to jonker street. The place is renovated from an old shophouse. Staff is polite and approachable. Overall value for money.

reviews.title: Great place to stay
reviews.text: The hotel is very asthetic and I had a pleasant time and stay there.

reviews.title: Great value, Excellent location
reviews.text:
Took at triple film foe me & the kids. Nice room,  nothing fancy but clean & comfortable.  Quiet room, strong AC.,
Read some complaints around the place being run down. True, some parts need some TLC… but this is Jonket Street, so it adds to the charm. Plus at these prices, it’s not gonna be the Ritz.
Essentially, you’re there for the location. Easy access to the car park, and just around the corner from the night market. You’re in the heart of the UNESCO heritage site, and a short walk to the old Dutch “Stadthuys”

reviews.title: Staff is helpful
reviews.text:
The street on this hotel is very busy. There are always on coming cars. Parking in the day is tough to get parking space on the street near the hotel, the staff is helpful to explain to me and showed me alternative parking under the hotel. 
The hotel’s own parking is a far walk at 2 streets at the back of the hotel. 

Very convenient walk around jonker street. The only set back is the clogged and flooded shower room, after showers.

reviews.title: Ideal location but...
reviews.text: It's an ideal location which is few min walk from the night market but not advisable for you to stay if you're a light sleeper cause can hear kids running on the corridor and ppl walking past. Got mosquito in my room and no proper lock for the room

reviews.title: Weekend in Melaka
reviews.text: Nice room, cold aircon which is great. Good location, off the main noisy crowded shopping night market street but near enough to walk to.

reviews.title: Great location
reviews.text:
We stayed at a bigger room that fits 8 people. Overall is good even though the furnitures are old but that's the main attraction.
Very near to famous restaurants nearby, in which we can go in odd hours to skip the long queue.

reviews.title: Excellent
reviews.text: Spent 3D2N there and had a great time. Staff were very friendly and helpful. Room was simple and clean in general. Only we found the pillows too flat and soft which kind of gave us neck ache. Parking is free in an open lot at the street behind the hotel which one has to access via the rear entrance but no biggie.

reviews.title: Travel with teenager and elderly mom
reviews.text: Ask location of parking as walking distance 2 streets behind from hotel. No escalator so take note for elderly persons.  Lovely hotel. Enjoyed the stay. Central location.

reviews.title: Excellent
reviews.text: The location is excellent, very near to Jonker Street so it's very easy and convenient to get around.  Staff are friendly and helpful. As we are travelling as a group, the room we stayed could be more roomy but it's still alright. We still enjoyed our stay.

reviews.title: near Jonker road!
reviews.text: Near Jonker road!

reviews.title: 2nd visit
reviews.text: 1st visit stayed at the suite which is nice. This time last min booked thus stayed in deluxe room G6. Looks old. Pillow too thin x 2 each. Very friendly staff. Will definitely be back but no more G6 room if can … location is good. Just turn left from hotel entrance for shortcut to Jonker street place :)

reviews.title: Nice upgraded Executive suite
reviews.text:
Spacious suite with friendly service and convenient location.
Should consider to provide advance info on the available free parking.

reviews.title: Excellent
reviews.text:
Location is easily accessible and near to honker street where the tourist attractions are. 

Free parking behind the hotel, around 2 mins walk. Call before check to arrange for parking. 

Room allocated is without window. Toilet design is inconvenient as it is not fully enclosed.  Odour from the toilet can be

reviews.title: Antique Hotel
reviews.text: Antique but class hotel. Beautifully designed, very clean,  good location...walking distance to whole Jongker Street. Most of all, very safe for Solo Traveler.

reviews.title: Hotel with nice interior and superb location
reviews.text:
The location of the hotel is nice, all of the tourist side and restaurant can reach by walking distance, also the interior view is awesome, their staff is super nice as well

The hotel has private parking but a little bit far from the hotel, and the parking space is quite limited, this is the only shortcoming I think

reviews.title: Great Nyonya heritage style hotel, really recommend if you loves heritage
reviews.text: Hotel is good and comfy and location is great. They have carpark just behind the hotel (We're lucky to get a parking just right behind).  But is a little old and there's some minor maintanence need to be done.

reviews.title: Great location and unique hotel
reviews.text:
We paid a bit more than usual as it was peak season but the hotel was centrally located and walkable to the main Melaka sights. Breakfast was delicious! Love all the Peranakan touches throughout the hotel. 
Would have liked for staff to recommend dining options that were more local but spoilt for choice in the location so it is not a deal breaker.

reviews.title: It's not a bad hotel but not impressive either
reviews.text:
I have to start with my complaints and end with my gratitude. (This is just my honest opinion)
It's has a very beautiful and welcoming lobby. The room isn't as nice as it is shown in the pictures on the booking website (maybe it's just the one that I got or I had the wrong expectations - it's rather subjective).  The room I got was a room with window but the window was non-functional (I like to have sunlight shinning into my room), it had to be kept closed all the time; this I could deal with it because it was only for 2 nights. 
The flooring on the second floor is really poor. My room is located on the ground floor. When, there is someone staying above, I could almost hear their every movement. It was made worse, when there was a family with 2 children staying in the room above.  
This hotel was located at close proximity to the famous jonker street, super easy access to all the nice eateries and cafes. The bed was comfortable. Staff working here was helpful and friendly.

reviews.title: Right in middle of Jonker
reviews.text: This is the third time ive been here. Hotel still as good as before, but a little more aged which adds to the charm. This time however I was travelling with a friend, and the open-concept bathroom made for some awkward moments. Do ask for an alternative room with closed off bathrooms if you are not travelling as a couple!

reviews.title: Nice Hotel near Jonkers Street
reviews.text: Hotel location was good.  However, there is very limited parking nearby and you might need to put your own parking coupon before you get a ticket. Near to Jonkers street.

reviews.title: Great location and friendly staff
reviews.text: definitely one of the best boutique hotel in Malacca. Walking distance to all tourist side and restaurants. Only down side is it can get noisy at night (from the pubs and traffic)  if you choose the main street facing rooms.

reviews.title: Convenient, clean, very nice design, nice staffs but wifi signal is weak in everywhere in Melaka
reviews.text: In overall we love the house and had a nice stay and enjoy Melaka :")

reviews.title: Nicely old fashioned
reviews.text: Great place for short stay recommended

reviews.title: Above average
reviews.text: We keep coming back to this hotel as its location is very good, just beside jonker street.

reviews.title: Ok
reviews.text: Book the big room, on picture was shown bed but when we reached, we are given platform beds instead. Rooms pictures not shown clearly thus misleading. The toilets are modern looking and clean. But the room walls and furniture seem quite worn. Big Lizards crawling and windows and corners very dusty not cleaned.

reviews.title: Good location. Peranakan style lodge at the central of Melaka historical city
reviews.text: The only thing that is not good enough is the parking. The nearest parking is 400m away. It is quite far when you have to carry big bags and walk under hot sun.

reviews.title: Boutique Hotel located very near Jonker Street
reviews.text: This is our second stay here. Last stay was in 2015. The rainshower/tap switch was not working properly and the glass door hinge had some issues during the second stay. I believe more can be done to upkeep this hotel to its former better days and condition.

reviews.title: Love this hotel!
reviews.text:
2nd time staying in Courtyard. 
Loved the quaint atmosphere, location and the friendly staff.

Only complaint is carpark is too far away. 
But we will be back again!

reviews.title: Great convenience with comfort.
reviews.text: Lovely boutique hotel with a surprising good breakfast!

reviews.title: 很美的酒店
reviews.text: 酒店很美很有特色，冷气不会冷，房间需要做一些维修和维护，墙壁有很多裂和破了，房间算很干净

reviews.title: Great mix of old meets modern
reviews.text: Lovely place and close to jonkers and lots of great places to shop for things.

reviews.title: Vintage Hotel
reviews.text: The hotel has a lovely vintage vibe and is just a short walk from Jonker Street and other attractions.

reviews.title: Good place for a short stay
reviews.text:
This is a boutique hotel, so you can't expect too much luxury. But this place exceeded my expectations. Very clean, very aesthetic with the traditional decor, and decently comfortable beds. The rooms had a very vintage/ grandma's house vibe to it. 
Unbeatable location.

reviews.title: Excellent
reviews.text: Very good location and value for money.

reviews.title: There are better choice
reviews.text: Water heater did not qork on the 2nd day, and air Con did not work on the 3rd day that we had to downgrade to another room.

reviews.title: Excellent
reviews.text: This is the second time we stayed here. However the first time experience was better as there was breakfast provided.

reviews.title: Very good location
reviews.text: Very good location

reviews.title: Love the environment
reviews.text: Spacious room and bathroom, my partner and me were enjoying and laughing a lot when one of us needs to make a big business. As bathroom is half opened concept, be ready for the fragrance :) I still like the room!!

reviews.title: Convenient location, friendly staff.
reviews.text: A quiet location just away from the bustling streets filled with tourists. A classic boutique hotel. The rooms are spacious and comfortable, but the bathrooms are not fully private, which might be a concern for some.

reviews.title: Strategic locations
reviews.text: Strategic locations, easy access to many cafe, restaurant and places. Cleanliness in the room moderate. Basic and simple amenities provided

reviews.title: Great Stay with Minor Issues
reviews.text: Fantastic walkability offered from the property, making it easy to explore the area. The room sizes are spacious and the staff is exceptionally friendly and helpful. Facilities are decent with some amenities needing improvement. Overall, a solid choice for a comfortable stay with a few minor drawbacks that don’t overshadow the positives.

reviews.title: Nice location, friendly staff
reviews.text:
Nice location, friendly staff, spacious room.
Do note, there is no lift in the hotel. However, staff will help with the luggage.
The only downside is the room is  old and poor maintain. The seats looks dirty, and the sockets is stuck out.

reviews.title: Good Location!!
reviews.text:
I had a wonderful stay at this hotel! The location is excellent, making it very convenient to explore the surrounding area. 

The staff were exceptionally friendly and welcoming, always ready to assist with a smile. My room was clean, comfortable, and well-maintained, contributing to a relaxing and enjoyable stay.

Overall, I highly recommend this hotel for anyone looking for a memorable and pleasant experience.

reviews.title: The location and atmosphere are excellent!
reviews.text: 5 minutes on foot from Dam Square. Most famous tourist spots can be visited on foot. The hotel, which is a renovated old building, has slightly outdated water facilities, but the atmosphere is wonderful, and I would highly recommend it. The security is also excellent. However, the key to the room I stayed in was difficult to open, and I would like that to be improved.

reviews.title: Modern Peranakan Style
reviews.text: If you love to explore the heritage area this must be the first choice! Must come during weekend as Jonker Street Night Market is open every Fri-Sun. Would suggest a little reno as no lock for the bathroom, bathroom clogged

reviews.title: Stay at one of Malacca Heritage Hotel
reviews.text:
I recently stayed at a charming heritage hotel in the heart of Malacca for one day and had a wonderful experience. The hotel’s prime location in the middle of town made it incredibly convenient, with all shops and marts just a short walk away. This central positioning allowed me to explore the local area with ease and truly immerse myself in the vibrant culture of Malacca.

The hotel itself exudes a rich historical vibe, with beautiful heritage decor that transports you back in time. The architecture and interior design are thoughtfully preserved, adding a unique charm and character to the stay. The room was clean, comfortable, and well-appointed, providing a perfect blend of modern amenities and old-world charm.

The staff were exceptionally friendly and accommodating, ensuring that all my needs were met promptly.

Overall, my brief stay at this heritage hotel was delightful. If you’re looking to experience the historical essence of Malacca while enjoying the convenience of being in the center of town, this hotel is an excellent choice. Highly recommended for a unique and memorable stay!

reviews.title: Exceptional location Pleasant stay
reviews.text: Had a pleasant stay at the heritage hotel. Exceptional location with nice cafe beside (The Baboon House).  Quiet environment. Walking distance to Jonker walk. Helpful staff to clear a clogged drainage at bathroom.

reviews.title: Every was great
reviews.text: Great room. could use more hot water for the showers...

reviews.title: Okay
reviews.text: Good. Thank you to the staff

reviews.title: Courtyard at heeren
reviews.text: Great space size and excellent location ;) staff there are helpful patient and kind

reviews.title: 3rd stay
reviews.text: This is my 3rd stay in the hotel. Love the ambience & atmosphere of this place. The receptionist, Intan & her colleagues are always very friendly & very helpful. The location is excellent.

reviews.title: Pleasant Stay
reviews.text: At the heart of Jonker Street, all main attractions are  walking distance. Overall experience is very nice.

reviews.title: Historic stay in a convenient location
reviews.text: Very conveniently located near Jonker street and easy access to prime tourist attractions and restaurants, a highly recommended place to stay. The hotel is in a historic Peranakan house and has lovely Peranakan decor. Room was nicely decorated in the Peranakan style.
